brands.name: American Standard
brands.notes: Sister brand, shares most platforms with Trane. Prefix 'A' typically = American Standard, 'T' = Trane

structural_overview: Trane Technologies residential gas furnaces span three distinct diagnostic generations with fundamentally different UX models: (1) LEGACY 2-DIGIT LED PLATFORMS — four separate public dictionaries (UD2/DD2 fan-assisted 2-stage, UC1/DC1 single-stage condensing, AUY/TUY 2-stage VS inducer, AUH2/TUH2 later 2-stage VS inducer) with SAME code numbers meaning DIFFERENT things across platforms. *UH1/*DH1 aliases to AUH2/TUH2. (2) S-SERIES 7-SEGMENT E-CODE — namespace redesign (not a carry-forward). Uses 7-segment display with menu items (IdL, L6F, C0d) plus alphanumeric alarm codes (e01, e2.1, e6.3). Covers S8X1/S8X2/S9X1/S9X2/S9B1/S9V2/S9V2-VS. (3) COMMUNICATING ECOSYSTEM — multi-surface architecture with THREE independent diagnostic surfaces: wall control (XL850/XL1050/XL824/AZON1050), outdoor CDA (Communicating Display Assembly on XV20i/XV18 variable-speed inverter), and indoor air handler display (TAM8/*AM8). Unlike Carrier's two-surface architecture, Trane uses a discovery-based fault model centered on Summary Table Enabled/Online/Offline states, with Err.126.00 as the primary 'device missing' indicator instead of Carrier's commissioning-time 'Indoor/Outdoor unit not found' codes. Public documentation coverage varies: strong for XL850/XL1050 wall control and TAM8/*AM8 air handler (Hyperion Field Reference Data); partial for XL824 and TEM8; weaker for outdoor alert booklet beyond publicly posted distributor PDFs; TEM6 and GAF2 do not expose communicating-bus alert namespaces publicly. No single master ComfortLink II alert dictionary exists in the public domain — techs must combine wall-control, outdoor-CDA, and equipment-specific sources.

field_quirks.quirk: Same code number means different things across Trane legacy platforms
field_quirks.applies_to: All four legacy 2-digit LED platforms (UD2/DD2, UC1/DC1, AUY/TUY, AUH2/TUH2)
field_quirks.reason: Code 9 means 'check ignitor circuit and N-to-24VAC common voltage' on UD2/DD2 and UC1/DC1, but means 'open inducer limit switch' on AUY/TUY and AUH2/TUH2 — completely different diagnostic paths. Code 6 varies across all four: UD2/DD2 = polarity+grounding only; UC1/DC1 adds 'system voltage too low'; AUY/TUY adds 'ignitor triac fault'; AUH2/TUH2 narrows back to 'line reverse polarity only'. Code 10 only exists on AUY/TUY as 'inducer communication error' — NOT on AUH2/TUH2. Platform identification via model prefix is mandatory before any code lookup.

field_quirks.quirk: S-series 7-segment is a namespace redesign, not a carry-forward from legacy flash codes
field_quirks.applies_to: S8X1, S8X2, S9X1, S9X2, S9B1, S9V2, S9V2-VS
field_quirks.reason: Legacy flash '4' = open limit maps conceptually to e-code 'e04' = open limit/rollout/reverse-airflow (broader). Legacy flash '6' = polarity/grounding bucket maps to e6.1 (polarity) + e6.2 (grounding) + e6.3 (igniter relay/open). A tech used to counting LED flashes will be completely lost on a 7-segment board. Use the 7-segment menu navigation (Menu button → L6F for last 6 faults) instead of visually counting.

field_quirks.quirk: Trane's communicating system uses three independent diagnostic surfaces, not one
field_quirks.applies_to: XV20i, XV18, TAM8/*AM8 with XL850/XL1050/XL824/AZON1050 wall control
field_quirks.reason: Unlike Carrier Infinity/Evolution's wall-control + equipment-board dual-surface model, Trane ComfortLink II adds a third surface: the outdoor CDA (Communicating Display Assembly) on variable-speed inverter outdoor units has its own Home/Monitor/Alert/Alert History/Configuration/Control menus. A fault can be visible at the wall control, the outdoor CDA, AND the indoor air handler display simultaneously — but each may show different levels of detail. Always check all three surfaces during communicating-system diagnosis.

field_quirks.quirk: ComfortLink II uses discovery-based fault detection via Summary Table, not commissioning codes
field_quirks.applies_to: XL850, XL1050, XL824, AZON1050 wall controls with any communicating equipment
field_quirks.reason: Rather than Carrier's 'Indoor unit not found' / 'Outdoor unit not found' commissioning codes, Trane shows Err.126.00 (Equipment Change Alert) when a discovered device stops reporting. Techs must consult the wall-control Summary Table (showing Enabled/Disabled + Online/Offline states per device) to diagnose. Enabled+Offline = Err.126.00 is the typical pattern. This shifts troubleshooting from 'what does this code mean' to 'which device went offline on the bus'.

field_quirks.quirk: TAM8/*AM8 air handler has multiple independent LEDs plus alphanumeric ERR codes
field_quirks.reason: Four LEDs provide diagnostic state: COMM LED (amber, flashes count of connected devices), BM LED (solid when powered), STATUS LED (flashes per 100 CFM during demand, 1/sec in idle), UNIT LED (blue, flashes every 4 seconds when comm is normal). Plus ERR codes on the thermostat display. Tech must read LEDs AND code together — either alone is incomplete. AFC STATUS LED solid-ON indicates certain faults independent of any numeric code.

field_quirks.quirk: 184.xx lockout family has different reset procedures on Legacy vs Link systems
field_quirks.reason: ERR.184.08 Universal Hard Lockout clears differently depending on generation: Legacy = cycle power at outdoor unit OR clear fault history via outdoor CDA; Link = reboot system from thermostat menu. Using the wrong reset procedure leaves the hard lockout in place even though underlying condition has cleared. Cycling power at disconnect does NOT always clear hard lockouts — check generation first.

field_quirks.quirk: Trane publishes alert severity labels (Critical/Major/Minor/User Display/Information) that affect UI presentation
field_quirks.applies_to: XL850, XL1050, TAM8/*AM8, some outdoor alerts
field_quirks.reason: Critical alerts show on wall-control home screen with red icon; Major alerts show with yellow icon but not on home screen; Minor alerts only appear in Diagnostics menu. User Display, Information, and lower severities may not even trigger customer-visible notifications. A homeowner may have an active fault they never saw. Always check Current Alerts menu during service, not just the home screen.

platforms.platform_description: Older 2-stage fan-assisted non-condensing furnace platform. Single LED on integrated furnace control (IFC) flashes 2-digit codes. Stores fault memory. Earliest of the four legacy Trane/AS dictionaries we document.
platforms.visual_identification: Single LED on furnace control board. Status states: Flashing Slow (normal no heat), Flashing Fast (normal with heat call), Continuous ON (replace IFC), Continuous OFF (check power). Codes 2-9 for faults.
platforms.platform_notes: Public installer guide 18-CD19D2-18 provides the code table. Code 9 means ignitor/N-to-common voltage check — different from AUY/TUY/AUH2/TUH2 Code 9 (inducer limit).

codes.meaning: Normal operation, no call for heat
diagnostic_path: Normal state — verify call-for-heat scenario if complaint is no heat
codes.source: Trane 18-CD19D2-18 Installer's Guide, p.23 code table

codes.meaning: Normal operation, call for heat active
diagnostic_path: Normal state during a heat call
codes.source: Trane 18-CD19D2-18 Installer's Guide, p.23

codes.meaning: Replace IFC (integrated furnace control)
diagnostic_path: If power is correct and LED remains solid on, replace IFC
codes.source: Trane 18-CD19D2-18 Installer's Guide, p.23

codes.meaning: Check power
codes.escalation: If power is present, IFC failure likely
codes.source: Trane 18-CD19D2-18 Installer's Guide, p.23

codes.meaning: System Lockout (Retries or Recycles exceeded)
diagnostic_path: Verify ignition sequence and flame sense
Public text says control retries up to 2 times after flame loss; then locks out if ignition not achieved
codes.reset_behavior: Manual reset by cycling power per lockout reset procedure.
codes.source: Trane 18-CD19D2-18 Installer's Guide, p.22-23

codes.meaning: Draft Pressure Error
diagnostic_path: Use three-way branch: venting path, pressure switch, inducer
Abnormal-condition text: if pressure not sensed, gas valve will not open; if failure during run, pressure switch causes shutdown
codes.escalation: Unit will not start or shuts down during run
codes.source: Trane 18-CD19D2-18 Installer's Guide, p.22-23

codes.meaning: Open High Limit Device
diagnostic_path: Check airflow and filter if limit is tripping
codes.source: Trane 18-CD19D2-18 Installer's Guide, p.23

codes.meaning: Flame sensed when no flame should be present
diagnostic_path: Shut off external gas and verify if code clears
If code clears, suspect leaking gas valve
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when false flame signal removed.
codes.source: Trane 18-CD19D2-18 Installer's Guide, p.23

codes.meaning: 115 Volt AC power reversed or Poor Grounding
diagnostic_path: Verify line polarity and grounding integrity
codes.source: Trane 18-CD19D2-18 Installer's Guide, p.23
codes.field_consensus_notes: CROSS-GENERATION CONFLICT: Code 6 meaning differs across all four Trane legacy platforms — UD2/DD2 here means polarity OR grounding; UC1/DC1 adds 'system voltage too low'; AUY/TUY adds 'ignitor triac fault'; AUH2/TUH2 narrows to 'line reverse polarity only'.

codes.meaning: Gas valve circuit error
diagnostic_path: Check gas valve wiring and circuit
codes.source: Trane 18-CD19D2-18 Installer's Guide, p.23

codes.meaning: Low flame sense signal
codes.escalation: Can contribute to recycle/lockout if flame proving fails
codes.source: Trane 18-CD19D2-18 Installer's Guide, p.23

codes.meaning: Check Ignitor circuit and Line N to 24VAC Common voltage (<2 volts) — possible grounding problem
root_causes: Line N to 24V common voltage relationship out of range
diagnostic_path: Measure Line N to 24VAC Common voltage (should be <2V)
codes.source: Trane 18-CD19D2-18 Installer's Guide, p.23
codes.field_consensus_notes: CROSS-GENERATION CONFLICT: Code 9 on UD2/DD2 and UC1/DC1 = ignitor/grounding; but Code 9 on AUY/TUY and AUH2/TUH2 = open inducer limit switch — completely different diagnosis.

platforms.platform_name: Trane/American Standard UC1/DC1 Legacy Single-Stage Condensing
platforms.platform_description: Older single-stage condensing furnace platform. Same 2-digit LED flash format as UD2/DD2 but with condensing-specific behaviors (condensate, burner-box limits, sealed combustion).
platforms.visual_identification: Single LED on furnace control board. Same status states as UD2/DD2 (Flashing Slow/Fast, Continuous ON/OFF). Codes 2-9 for faults. Burner-box manual-reset thermal limit distinguishes this platform from UD2/DD2 in troubleshooting path.
platforms.platform_notes: Public installer guide 18-CD19D5-21 provides the code table with explicit off-on-off-on within 30 seconds reset procedure. Code 4 focus includes burner-box manual-reset limit.

codes.meaning: Normal, no call for heat
codes.source: Trane 18-CD19D5-21 Installer's Guide, p.35-37

codes.meaning: Normal, call for heat active
codes.source: Trane 18-CD19D5-21 Installer's Guide, p.35-37

codes.meaning: Replace IFC
diagnostic_path: Verify power; if present, replace IFC
codes.source: Trane 18-CD19D5-21 Installer's Guide, p.35-37

codes.meaning: Check power
diagnostic_path: Verify line voltage, 24V, door switch, transformer
codes.escalation: If power present, IFC failure likely
codes.source: Trane 18-CD19D5-21 Installer's Guide, p.35-37

codes.meaning: System Lockout (Retries or Recycles exceeded)
diagnostic_path: Verify gas and ignition; public text says control recycles and locks out if ignition not achieved
codes.reset_behavior: Reset requires off-on-off-on within 30 seconds at unit power source or thermostat.
codes.source: Trane 18-CD19D5-21 Installer's Guide, p.35-37

codes.meaning: Draft Pressure Error
diagnostic_path: Use three-branch check: venting, pressure switch, inducer
Also inspect condensate path on this condensing platform
codes.escalation: No start or shutdown during run
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after fault correction; lockout reset with off-on-off-on within 30 sec if full lockout occurred.
codes.source: Trane 18-CD19D5-21 Installer's Guide, p.35-37

codes.meaning: Open Temperature Limit Circuit
diagnostic_path: Check vent and combustion-air terminations for blockage
codes.reset_behavior: Burner-box manual-reset limit can be reset after blockage cleared; otherwise qualified servicer reset.
codes.source: Trane 18-CD19D5-21 Installer's Guide, p.35-37

codes.meaning: Flame sensed when no flame should be present
diagnostic_path: Shut off gas and verify if code clears
codes.source: Trane 18-CD19D5-21 Installer's Guide, p.37

codes.meaning: 115 volt AC power reversed, poor grounding, or system voltage too low
diagnostic_path: Verify polarity, grounding, and system voltage
codes.source: Trane 18-CD19D5-21 Installer's Guide, p.37
codes.field_consensus_notes: CROSS-GENERATION CONFLICT: UC1/DC1 Code 6 uniquely adds 'system voltage too low' vs UD2/DD2 (polarity/grounding only), AUY/TUY (adds triac), AUH2/TUH2 (polarity only).

codes.meaning: Gas valve circuit error
codes.source: Trane 18-CD19D5-21 Installer's Guide, p.37

codes.meaning: Low flame sense signal
codes.source: Trane 18-CD19D5-21 Installer's Guide, p.37

codes.meaning: Check Ignitor Circuit and Line N to 24VAC Common voltage (≤2 volts) — possible grounding problem
diagnostic_path: Measure Line N to 24VAC Common (should be ≤2V)
codes.source: Trane 18-CD19D5-21 Installer's Guide, p.37
codes.field_consensus_notes: CROSS-GENERATION CONFLICT: UC1/DC1 Code 9 = ignitor/grounding (same as UD2/DD2); but AUY/TUY and AUH2/TUH2 Code 9 = open inducer limit.

platforms.platform_name: Trane/American Standard AUY/TUY/ADY/TDY Legacy 2-Stage Condensing with Variable-Speed Inducer
platforms.platform_description: Older 2-stage condensing variable-speed-inducer platform using separate red/green LEDs. Introduced inducer-communication logic and solid-red/solid-green diagnostic states. Last-4-fault memory with R-to-W1 erase sequence.
platforms.visual_identification: Dual-LED design (red + green). Green slow flash = Normal no heat; Green fast flash = Normal call for heat. Red LED flashes fault codes 2-10. Last 4 faults replay on red LED at power-up. Solid-red codes do NOT replay in last-fault recovery. Solid Red + Solid Green = continuous reset (blown fuse or internal error).
platforms.platform_notes: Public installer guide 18-CD20D3-21 covers the platform. Last-4-fault erase: power up with G energized, apply R to W1 three times within 6 seconds; red LED turns on 2 seconds to acknowledge reset. Lockout reset: turn system power off >1 second, or remove 24VAC >1 second, or remove thermostat heat call >1 second but <20 seconds.

codes.meaning: Normal, no call for heat
codes.source: Trane 18-CD20D3-21 Installer's Guide, p.36

codes.meaning: Normal, call for heat active
codes.source: Trane 18-CD20D3-21 Installer's Guide, p.36

codes.meaning: System Lockout (Retries or Recycles exceeded)
root_causes: Retry/recycle lockout from ignition failures or flame loss
diagnostic_path: Verify gas supply and ignition sequence
Flame loss causes recycle then lockout if ignition not achieved
codes.reset_behavior: Reset by turning system power off >1 second, or removing 24VAC >1 second, or removing thermostat heat call >1 second but <20 seconds.
codes.source: Trane 18-CD20D3-21 Installer's Guide, p.35-36

codes.meaning: Draft Pressure Error
diagnostic_path: Three-branch check: venting, pressure switch, inducer
codes.escalation: Shutdown / no start depending on when fault occurs
codes.source: Trane 18-CD20D3-21 Installer's Guide, p.36

codes.meaning: Open Temperature Limit Switch
diagnostic_path: Inspect burner-box limit and vent/air terminations
codes.reset_behavior: Burner-box limit may be manually reset after blockage cleared.
codes.source: Trane 18-CD20D3-21 Installer's Guide, p.35-36

codes.meaning: Flame sensed when no flame should be present
diagnostic_path: Shut off gas and verify if code clears
codes.source: Trane 18-CD20D3-21 Installer's Guide, p.36

codes.meaning: 115 volt AC power reversed, ignitor (Triac) fault, poor grounding, or system voltage too low
diagnostic_path: Verify polarity, ignitor/triac condition, grounding, system voltage
codes.source: Trane 18-CD20D3-21 Installer's Guide, p.36
codes.field_consensus_notes: CROSS-GENERATION CONFLICT: AUY/TUY Code 6 uniquely adds 'ignitor triac fault' vs UD2/DD2 (polarity/grounding only), UC1/DC1 (adds low voltage), AUH2/TUH2 (polarity only).

codes.meaning: Gas valve circuit error
codes.source: Trane 18-CD20D3-21 Installer's Guide, p.36

codes.meaning: Low flame sense
codes.source: Trane 18-CD20D3-21 Installer's Guide, p.36

codes.meaning: Open Inducer Limit switch
diagnostic_path: Public abnormal-condition text also points to variable-speed draft inducer troubleshooting manual
codes.source: Trane 18-CD20D3-21 Installer's Guide, p.35-36
codes.field_consensus_notes: CROSS-GENERATION CONFLICT: Code 9 on AUY/TUY = inducer limit; but Code 9 on UD2/DD2 and UC1/DC1 = ignitor/grounding check.

codes.meaning: Inducer communication error
root_causes: Communication error involving inducer assembly / control path
diagnostic_path: Refer to variable-speed draft inducer troubleshooting manual per OEM
codes.source: Trane 18-CD20D3-21 Installer's Guide, p.35-36
codes.field_consensus_notes: CROSS-GENERATION CONFLICT: Code 10 exists ONLY on AUY/TUY in the public dictionary. AUH2/TUH2 does NOT publish a Code 10 entry.

codes.meaning: Internal GV error or Low TH voltage
diagnostic_path: Verify thermostat voltage and internal control state
codes.source: Trane 18-CD20D3-21 Installer's Guide, p.36

codes.meaning: Continuous Reset caused by a blown fuse or internal error
codes.reset_behavior: Solid-red codes are not displayed during fault-code recovery replay.
codes.source: Trane 18-CD20D3-21 Installer's Guide, p.36

platforms.platform_name: Trane/American Standard AUH2/TUH2/ADH2/TDH2 + *UH1/*DH1 Later Legacy 2-Stage Condensing with VS Inducer
platforms.platform_description: Later legacy 2-stage condensing variable-speed-inducer platform. Simplified dictionary vs AUY/TUY — no Code 10, Code 6 narrowed to polarity only, Code 3 now explicitly 'Inducer OR Pressure Switch Error' with stage-dependent retry behavior. The *UH1/*DH1 platform aliases to this dictionary (public guide 18-CD29D1-14-EN has no separate flash-code namespace) — same codes, same status indicators.
platforms.visual_identification: Dual-LED design (green + red). Green slow flash = Normal no heat; Green fast flash = Normal with heat call. Red LED for fault codes 2-9. Last 4 faults replay on red LED at power-up. Green + Red continuous ON = fuse open or internal control failure.
platforms.platform_notes: Public installer guides 18-CD26D1-13 (AUH2) and 18-CD29D1-14-EN (*UH1) share the same dictionary. Same last-4-fault erase: power up with G energized, apply R to W1 three times within 6 seconds; red LED acknowledges for 2 seconds. Lockout reset same as AUY/TUY: power off >1s, or remove 24VAC >1s, or remove heat call >1s but <20s.

codes.meaning: Normal, no call for heat
codes.source: Trane 18-CD26D1-13 Installer's Guide, p.42-43

codes.meaning: Normal, call for heat active
codes.source: Trane 18-CD26D1-13 Installer's Guide, p.42-43

codes.meaning: Retries or recycles exceeded
root_causes: Ignition retries exceeded or flame-loss recycles exceeded
diagnostic_path: Verify gas supply and ignition sequence
codes.reset_behavior: Reset by power off >1s, or remove 24VAC >1s, or remove thermostat heat call >1s but <20s.
codes.source: Trane 18-CD26D1-13 Installer's Guide, p.42-43

codes.meaning: Inducer or Pressure Switch Error
codes.escalation: In 2nd stage: inducer fault forces transition to 1st stage for 10 minutes before retry. In 1st stage: system shuts down and waits 30 seconds before attempting first-stage reestablishment.
codes.reset_behavior: Automatic retry per above; full lockout reset if needed.
codes.source: Trane 18-CD26D1-13 Installer's Guide, p.42-43

codes.meaning: Open Limit or Rollout Switch
codes.reset_behavior: Manual-reset burner-box limit after blockage correction, or qualified-servicer reset.
codes.source: Trane 18-CD26D1-13 Installer's Guide, p.42-43

codes.meaning: Flame sensed when no flame should be present
diagnostic_path: Shut off gas and verify if code clears
codes.source: Trane 18-CD26D1-13 Installer's Guide, p.43

codes.meaning: Line Reverse Polarity
codes.source: Trane 18-CD26D1-13 Installer's Guide, p.43
codes.field_consensus_notes: CROSS-GENERATION CONFLICT: AUH2/TUH2 Code 6 is NARROWER than predecessors — polarity only. AUY/TUY Code 6 also covered ignitor triac fault, grounding, and low voltage; UD2/DD2 and UC1/DC1 Code 6 also covered grounding.

codes.meaning: Gas Valve Circuit Error
codes.source: Trane 18-CD26D1-13 Installer's Guide, p.43

codes.meaning: Weak Flame
codes.source: Trane 18-CD26D1-13 Installer's Guide, p.43

codes.meaning: Open Inducer Limit Error
diagnostic_path: Inducer retry behavior documented in abnormal-condition section
codes.escalation: Heating fault; stage fallback/retry behavior documented separately
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when inducer limit condition clears.
codes.source: Trane 18-CD26D1-13 Installer's Guide, p.42-43
codes.field_consensus_notes: CROSS-GENERATION CONFLICT: Code 9 on AUH2/TUH2 = inducer limit (same as AUY/TUY); but Code 9 on UD2/DD2 and UC1/DC1 = ignitor/grounding check.

codes.meaning: Fuse open or internal control failure
diagnostic_path: Replace control if fuse tests good and fault persists
codes.reset_behavior: Not replayed as a solid-red fault in last-fault recovery.
codes.source: Trane 18-CD26D1-13 Installer's Guide, p.43

platforms.platform_description: Current-generation Trane/AS residential gas furnace platform using 7-segment alphanumeric display. Complete namespace redesign from legacy flash-count boards — NOT a carry-forward. Uses menu system (Menu button → IdL, L6F, C0d, etc.) plus alphanumeric alarm codes (e01, e2.1, e04, e6.1). Covers S8X1/S8X2 non-condensing, S9X1/S9X2/S9B1 condensing high-efficiency-motor, S9V2 variable-speed, and S9V2-VS variable-speed-inducer. Shared code namespace across these model lines with minor branch-specific differences (e3.3/e3.4 only on 2-stage S8X2-type logic).
platforms.visual_identification: 7-segment alphanumeric display showing menu items (IdL = idle, L6F = last 6 faults menu, C0d = cooling off delay, etc.) and alarm codes (e01, e2.1, e3.1, e04, e05, e6.1, e6.2, e6.3, e7.1, e08, e11, e12). Menu and Option buttons for navigation. Last 6 faults viewable via L6F; clear by holding Option 5+ seconds (shows three dashes three times). Factory reset: hold Menu + Option 15 seconds (display flashes FD three times).
platforms.platform_notes: Public guides: 18-CE04D1-1D-EN (S8X1/S8X2), 18-CE12D1-1D-EN (S9X2/S9B1), 18-CE19D1-1B-EN (S9V2), 18-CE03D1-1F-EN (S9V2-VS). e3.3 and e3.4 are 2-stage pressure-switch-only codes (S8X2 family). Menu/status items (IdL, L6F, etc.) are NOT faults — they are display states and service menu labels.

codes.meaning: Idle state — furnace is powered and idle with no active call
codes.source: S-series 7-segment IFC display code pages

codes.meaning: Active alarm menu — use Option button to navigate active alarms
diagnostic_path: Press Option to view active alarm codes
codes.source: S-series 7-segment IFC display code pages

codes.meaning: Last 6 faults menu — historical fault recall
diagnostic_path: Press Menu until L6F displays, enter with Option, scroll last six faults
To clear: enter L6F, hold Option at least 5 seconds; display shows three dashes three times
codes.source: S-series 7-segment IFC display code pages

codes.meaning: Code release number (firmware version identifier)
diagnostic_path: Normal service-menu label — displays firmware version
codes.source: S-series 7-segment IFC display code pages

codes.meaning: Cooling off delay — service menu for cooling-off-delay setting
codes.source: S-series 7-segment IFC display code pages

codes.meaning: Outdoor unit type — service menu for outdoor unit type selection
codes.source: S-series 7-segment IFC display code pages

codes.meaning: Blower constant fan airflow — service menu for constant-fan CFM setting
codes.source: S-series 7-segment IFC display code pages

codes.meaning: Heat off delay — service menu for heat-off-delay setting
codes.source: S-series 7-segment IFC display code pages

codes.meaning: Inter-stage delay — service menu for staging delay setting
codes.source: S-series 7-segment IFC display code pages

codes.meaning: Gas heating CFM — service menu for gas-heat airflow setting
codes.source: S-series 7-segment IFC display code pages

codes.meaning: Test mode / run menu context
codes.source: S-series 7-segment IFC display code pages

codes.meaning: Loss of IRQ or other internal failures (Internal IFC error)
codes.escalation: Control fault — no normal heat sequence
codes.reset_behavior: Active-alarm display clears only when condition clears. Last-6-fault memory cleared separately via L6F.
codes.source: Trane 18-CE04D1-1D-EN, display codes page 58

codes.meaning: Retry Exceeded — flame never sensed
root_causes: No flame established during ignition trials
diagnostic_path: Verify flame sense current and ground
codes.reset_behavior: One-hour lockout per public table; historical fault handled through L6F.
codes.source: Trane 18-CE04D1-1D-EN, code table page 58

codes.meaning: Recycles Exceeded — flame sensed then lost
root_causes: Flame established then lost during run
diagnostic_path: Measure flame current under run conditions
codes.source: Trane 18-CE04D1-1D-EN, code table page 58

codes.meaning: Flame current is low, but still strong enough to allow operation
codes.source: Trane 18-CE04D1-1D-EN, code table page 58

codes.meaning: Shorted Pressure Switch, 1st Stage
diagnostic_path: Verify switch state before inducer starts
If PS1 does not close within 60 seconds, control reports e3.1 and increases inducer to high speed to try to close PS1
codes.source: Trane 18-CE04D1-1D-EN, code table page 58 and sequence pages 60-61

codes.meaning: Open Pressure Switch, 1st Stage
codes.escalation: Heat sequence interrupted until switch state correct
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when pressure switch closes correctly.
codes.source: Trane 18-CE04D1-1D-EN, code table page 58

codes.meaning: Shorted Pressure Switch, 2nd Stage (S8X2 2-stage only)
codes.source: Trane 18-CE04D1-1D-EN, code table page 58
codes.field_consensus_notes: Branch-specific: present only on 2-stage S8X2-type logic.

codes.meaning: Open Pressure Switch, 2nd Stage (S8X2 2-stage only)
diagnostic_path: If PS2 does not close within 45 seconds, PS2-open error is declared and furnace operates in 1 stage
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when PS2 proves closed correctly.
codes.source: Trane 18-CE04D1-1D-EN, code table page 58 and sequence pages 60-61
codes.field_consensus_notes: Branch-specific: present only on 2-stage S8X2-type logic.

codes.meaning: Open Limit (Main Thermal, Rollout Switch, or Reverse Airflow Switch)
diagnostic_path: Identify which device in the safety chain is open
Check airflow, filter, blower if main limit
Check burners, venting, HX if rollout
Check blower direction/wiring if reverse airflow
codes.reset_behavior: Depends on device; clears when safety circuit restores. Manual-reset devices require manual reset after correction.
codes.source: Trane 18-CE12D1-1D-EN code-table snippet; same namespace in 18-CE04D1-1D-EN
codes.field_consensus_notes: CROSS-GENERATION CONFLICT: Legacy Code 4 = open limit; e04 broadens to include rollout AND reverse-airflow switch. Diagnostic path must branch on which device opened.

codes.meaning: Flame detected, should not be present
diagnostic_path: Shut off gas and verify if code clears
codes.source: Trane 18-CE12D1-1D-EN code table; same namespace in 18-CE04D1-1D-EN

codes.meaning: Voltage reversed polarity
codes.source: Trane 18-CE12D1-1D-EN code table; same namespace in 18-CE04D1-1D-EN
codes.field_consensus_notes: CROSS-GENERATION CONFLICT: Legacy Code 6 was a combined polarity/grounding/low-voltage/triac bucket. E-code generation splits into e6.1 (polarity only), e6.2 (grounding only), e6.3 (igniter relay/open). Different diagnostic paths for each subcode.

codes.meaning: Bad grounding
codes.source: Trane 18-CE12D1-1D-EN code table; same namespace in 18-CE04D1-1D-EN

codes.meaning: Igniter relay fails OR igniter open
diagnostic_path: Verify igniter relay operation on board
codes.source: Trane 18-CE04D1-1D-EN, code table page 58

codes.meaning: 1st stage gas valve (MVL) is energized when it should be off
root_causes: 1st-stage gas valve energized when it should be off
diagnostic_path: Inspect for backfeed or control fault
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when gas-valve control fault corrected.
codes.source: Trane 18-CE04D1-1D-EN, code table page 58

codes.meaning: Flame current is low, but still strong enough to allow operation
codes.source: Trane 18-CE12D1-1D-EN code table

codes.meaning: Gas valve relay/control fault — 5 branches: (1) 1st stage gas valve not energized when it should be, (2) 1st stage gas valve relay stuck closed, (3) 2nd stage gas valve relay stuck closed, (4) 2nd stage gas valve energized when it should not be, (5) 2nd stage gas valve not energized when it should be
root_causes: Gas-valve relay fault on 1st or 2nd stage
diagnostic_path: Verify gas valve wiring to schematic
Test both 1st and 2nd stage gas-valve relays on board
Verify no shorts or backfeed in gas-valve circuit
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when valve/control fault corrected. Historical entry remains in L6F until cleared.
codes.source: Trane 18-CE04D1-1D-EN, code table page 58

codes.meaning: Open fuse
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when fuse replaced and short corrected. Fault memory remains in L6F until cleared.
codes.source: Trane 18-CE12D1-1D-EN, code table page 109; same in 18-CE04D1-1D-EN page 58

platforms.platform_name: Trane ComfortLink II / American Standard AccuLink Wall Controls
platforms.platform_description: ComfortLink II / AccuLink communicating wall controls (XL850, XL1050, XL824, AccuLink Platinum 1050/AZON1050). Multi-touch color displays with discovered-device Summary Table, Current Alerts (active), Alert History (cleared alerts from last 30 days), and severity-tagged alerts (Critical/Major/Minor). Critical alerts appear on home screen (red icon); Major alerts appear with yellow icon but NOT on home screen; Minor alerts only appear in Diagnostics.
platforms.visual_identification: Color touchscreen wall control. Shows Current Alerts and Alert History in Diagnostics screen. Discovered-device Summary Table lists all communicating devices on bus with Enabled/Disabled and Online/Offline states. Severity badges: red icon (Critical), yellow icon (Major), in-menu-only (Minor).
platforms.platform_notes: Public documentation: 18-HD80D1-6F-EN (XL1050), 18-HD73D1-9E-EN (XL850), 11-HD15D1-5-EN (AccuLink Platinum 1050). XL824 alert dictionary is thinner in public sources than XL850/XL1050. The wall-control alert surface is FRAGMENTED — it shows system-level alerts like Err.126.00 but passes through equipment-level codes from outdoor CDA and indoor air handler. No single OEM booklet covers the complete wall-control dictionary.

codes.meaning: Active alerts menu — shows currently active system alerts
diagnostic_path: Diagnostics menu → Current Alerts to view active alerts

codes.meaning: Historical alerts from last 30 days — stores cleared alerts
diagnostic_path: Diagnostics menu → Alert History to review cleared alerts

codes.meaning: Discovered-device summary showing all communicating devices with Enabled/Disabled and Online/Offline states
diagnostic_path: Use Summary Table to identify which device(s) are offline or unexpectedly disabled
Offline devices that are Enabled typically trigger Err.126.00
codes.source: XL1050 18-HD80D1-6F-EN, XL850 18-HD73D1-9E-EN, AccuLink 11-HD15D1-5-EN

codes.meaning: Factory default reset menu
diagnostic_path: Service menu label — only use intentionally during troubleshooting or replacement

codes.meaning: Initial setup / commissioning wizard
diagnostic_path: Used during initial system setup or after factory reset

codes.meaning: Device state indicators in Summary Table — Online = communicating normally; Offline = not reporting; Enabled = system expects device on bus; Disabled = system ignores device
diagnostic_path: Enabled + Offline = typically triggers Err.126.00 Equipment Change Alert
Disabled + Offline = normal (device intentionally not in use)

codes.meaning: Equipment Change Alert — equipment reporting does not match equipment originally discovered; enabled device is offline or not reporting on bus
root_causes: Communicating device not reporting on bus
Power flicker to device (equipment stopped reporting)
Failed communicating board on a discovered device
diagnostic_path: Check wall-control Summary Table to identify which device is offline
If outdoor unit offline and heat pump not running (system falls back to furnace/electric heat): verify outdoor power, comm wiring, and board state
Cycle/restore reporting status of missing device
If device was removed intentionally, set to Disabled in Summary Table
codes.escalation: On heat-pump systems: compressor operation may be lost, system may fall back to furnace or electric heat only
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when missing/offline device returns to reporting status on the bus. No unique reset button — alert self-clears on device reconnection.
codes.source: XL1050 18-HD80D1-6F-EN Diagnostics/Summary Table, XL850 18-HD73D1-9E-EN Diagnostics, AccuLink Platinum 1050 11-HD15D1-5-EN diagnostics troubleshooting, Trane System Alert Guide public distributor PDF p.25
codes.field_consensus_notes: Primary 'device missing' indicator in Trane ecosystem — analog to Carrier 'Indoor/Outdoor unit not found' commissioning codes but with different architecture. Read it through Summary Table, not as a standalone slogan. Often indicates outdoor unit offline after power flicker.

platforms.research_note: Trane communicating platforms use structured alert IDs (CFG.005.01, ERR.188.05 format). Public OEM dictionary was not fully extractable in Pass 1 research — authoritative source is the Trane Technician app. Techs should consult app for detailed subcode troubleshooting.

platforms.platform_name: Trane XV20i / XV18 / American Standard Platinum Variable-Speed Communicating Outdoor
platforms.platform_description: Communicating variable-speed outdoor inverter platform using Communicating Display Assembly (CDA) as local diagnostic surface. Covers XV20i (4TWV0 family, TruComfort variable-speed modulating), XV18 (4TWV8 family, TruComfort variable-speed two-stage), American Standard AccuComfort Platinum variable-speed siblings. Outdoor faults visible at the wall control AND at the outdoor CDA. 184.xx family is drive-protection; 185.xx family is drive-modulation; 068.xx is defrost; 126.00 is system-level equipment-change. Lockouts escalate from soft (5-15 min) to Universal Hard Lockout (184.08).
platforms.visual_identification: Outdoor Communicating Display Assembly (CDA) inside heat pump control box. Home/Status screen + Monitor Menu + Alert Menu + Alert History + Configuration Menu + Control Menu. CDA displays alert codes locally AND codes are passed to wall control. COMM light behavior independent of CDA screen. Variable-speed modulating compressor distinguishes this platform from two-stage communicating outdoor.
platforms.platform_notes: Public source: Trane System Alert Guide (TR_Alarm-Guide-Booklet_8-5-2020.pdf) public distributor-posted PDF. Pages 18 (068.xx defrost), 25 (126.00 equipment change), 41 (184.xx drive protection), 42 (185.xx drive modulation). 184.08 Universal Hard Lockout has DIFFERENT reset procedures on Legacy (cycle power or clear CDA fault history) vs Link (thermostat reboot menu). 184.07 is separate Max Protection Hard Lockout trigger.

codes.meaning: Outdoor CDA real-time status menu
diagnostic_path: Use Monitor Menu to observe live system state, suction pressure, superheat, etc.
codes.source: Trane XV20i/XV18 installer/service documentation

codes.meaning: Outdoor CDA active alerts menu
diagnostic_path: Use Alert Menu to view active outdoor faults at CDA
codes.source: Trane XV20i/XV18 installer/service documentation

codes.meaning: Outdoor CDA historical faults list
diagnostic_path: Use to review past outdoor faults at CDA
On Legacy systems, clearing CDA fault history is part of 184.08 hard-lockout reset procedure
codes.reset_behavior: Menu label. Can be cleared as part of hard-lockout reset on Legacy systems.
codes.source: Trane XV20i/XV18 installer/service documentation

codes.meaning: Outdoor CDA configuration/setup menu
codes.source: Trane XV20i/XV18 installer/service documentation

codes.meaning: Outdoor CDA test/control functions menu
diagnostic_path: Service-only menu for forced defrost, compressor tests, etc.
codes.source: Trane XV20i/XV18 installer/service documentation

codes.meaning: Active defrost cycle indicator shown on CDA screen
diagnostic_path: Normal state during defrost — not a fault
codes.source: Trane XV20i/XV18 installer/service documentation

codes.meaning: Shown on CDA after completion of certain tests/diagnostics
codes.source: Trane XV20i/XV18 installer/service documentation

codes.meaning: Defrost Fault B and/or C
root_causes: Defrost terminated at least 10 times in a row on maximum defrost time
diagnostic_path: Check refrigerant charge and coil condition
Check outdoor fan operation during defrost
codes.escalation: Not explicitly exposed in the accessible public snippet
codes.reset_behavior: Not explicitly exposed in public snippet.
codes.source: Trane System Alert Guide public distributor-posted PDF, 068.xx family (page 18 for related snippet)

codes.meaning: Defrost Fault A and (B and/or C) — combined defrost-fault condition
root_causes: Outdoor fan running during defrost when it should not be
diagnostic_path: Verify outdoor fan operation during defrost
Check defrost sensor and reversing valve
codes.escalation: Not explicitly exposed in accessible public snippet
codes.reset_behavior: Not explicitly exposed in public snippet.
codes.source: Trane System Alert Guide public distributor-posted PDF p.18

codes.meaning: Compressor Dome Temperature Protection — High Speed
root_causes: Indoor expansion valve in cooling mode
Outdoor expansion valve in heating mode
High indoor ambient in heat mode
codes.escalation: 15-minute soft lockout; 5 occurrences within approximately 5 hours result in Universal Hard Lockout (184.08)
codes.reset_behavior: Soft lockout clears after timer; repeated occurrences escalate to 184.08 hard lockout.
codes.source: Trane System Alert Guide p.41, public distributor-posted PDF

codes.meaning: Compressor Dome Temperature Protection — Low Speed
root_causes: Indoor expansion valve in cooling mode
Outdoor expansion valve in heating mode
High indoor ambient in heat mode
diagnostic_path: Same diagnostic path as 184.01 but triggered at low speed
codes.escalation: 15-minute soft lockout; 5 occurrences within approximately 5 hours result in Universal Hard Lockout (184.08)
codes.reset_behavior: Soft lockout clears after timer; repeated occurrences escalate.
codes.source: Trane System Alert Guide p.41, public distributor-posted PDF

codes.meaning: Low Suction Pressure Cooling Protection
root_causes: Pressure transducer out of calibration or failure
codes.reset_behavior: Soft lockout clears after timer; repeated 184.xx events can escalate to hard lockout.
codes.source: Trane System Alert Guide p.41, public distributor-posted PDF

codes.meaning: Low Suction Pressure Heating Protection
codes.reset_behavior: Soft lockout clears; repeated events escalate.
codes.source: Trane System Alert Guide p.41, public distributor-posted PDF

codes.meaning: Max Current Limit Protection
root_causes: Drive has reached maximum allowed current at low-speed operation
diagnostic_path: Public System Alert Guide snippet confirms the protection event
Deeper cause tree in full alert booklet/TSM
codes.escalation: 5-minute soft lockout; 5 occurrences within approximately 5 hours result in Universal Hard Lockout
codes.reset_behavior: Soft lockout clears after timer; repeated escalate.
codes.source: Trane System Alert Guide public distributor-posted PDF

codes.meaning: Diagonal Current Protection
codes.escalation: 5-minute soft lockout; 5 occurrences within approximately 5 hours result in Universal Hard Lockout (184.08)
codes.source: Trane System Alert Guide p.41, public distributor-posted PDF

codes.meaning: Max Protection Hard Lockout
root_causes: Maximum number of protection shutdowns (184.xx) have occurred
diagnostic_path: Review preceding 184.xx fault history to identify root cause of repeated protection events
codes.reset_behavior: Clearing procedure per 184.08: Legacy = cycle power to outdoor unit OR clear fault history in outdoor CDA; Link = thermostat reboot menu. Power cycling at disconnect alone may not clear.
codes.source: Trane System Alert Guide p.41, public distributor-posted PDF
codes.field_consensus_notes: Separate hard-lockout trigger from 184.08. Same reset procedure but triggered by different threshold.

codes.meaning: Universal Hard Lockout — system entered hard lockout after repeated protection shutdowns
root_causes: Repeated 184.xx protection events reached universal hard-lockout threshold
diagnostic_path: Investigate alerts leading to the hard lockout
Legacy systems: cycle power to outdoor unit OR clear fault history in outdoor CDA
Link systems: reboot system from thermostat menu
codes.reset_behavior: LEGACY: cycle power to outdoor unit or clear fault history in outdoor CDA. LINK: reboot system from thermostat menu. DIFFERENT PROCEDURES — use the one matching your system generation.
codes.source: Trane System Alert Guide p.41, public distributor-posted PDF
codes.field_consensus_notes: CRITICAL: Reset procedure differs Legacy vs Link. Using the wrong procedure leaves the hard lockout in place even though underlying condition has cleared.

codes.meaning: Internal Lube Failure
root_causes: Three consecutive internal lube cycles were missed
Internal communication fault within drive software
diagnostic_path: Drive will reboot to reset software code — no field action required per public snippet
codes.reset_behavior: Drive will reboot to reset software code.
codes.source: Trane System Alert Guide p.41, public distributor-posted PDF

codes.meaning: SOV (switch-over valve) in Wrong Mode
root_causes: Switch-over valve is in heating mode while system is calling for cooling
Outdated outdoor VSPD software / thermostat software
diagnostic_path: Reprogram drive to latest outdoor-unit VSPD software
Check SOV wiring and connections to drive
codes.source: Trane System Alert Guide p.41, public distributor-posted PDF

codes.meaning: Compressor Dome Temp Protect Down (speed reduction response)
diagnostic_path: Check dome temperature and ambient conditions
Fuller cause tree in full alert booklet
codes.reset_behavior: Not explicitly exposed in accessible snippet.
codes.source: Trane System Alert Guide p.42, public distributor-posted PDF

codes.meaning: Compressor Dome Temp Protect Up (speed increase response)
codes.reset_behavior: Not explicitly exposed in accessible snippet.
codes.source: Trane System Alert Guide p.42, public distributor-posted PDF

codes.meaning: Compressor Dome Temp Protect — Low Hold
diagnostic_path: Hold response version of low-speed dome-temperature control
codes.reset_behavior: Not explicitly exposed in accessible snippet.
codes.source: Trane System Alert Guide p.42, public distributor-posted PDF

codes.meaning: Compressor Lubrication Active
codes.escalation: Not explicitly exposed in accessible snippet
codes.source: Trane System Alert Guide p.42, public distributor-posted PDF

codes.meaning: Drive Current Protect Up
root_causes: High compressor load during low-speed operation
diagnostic_path: Check compressor load and system conditions causing high current draw
codes.reset_behavior: Not cleanly documented in public snippet.
codes.source: Trane System Alert Guide public distributor-posted PDF

codes.meaning: Drive Current Protect Hold
codes.source: Trane System Alert Guide p.42, public distributor-posted PDF

codes.meaning: System Oil Return Active
root_causes: System Oil Return function active to bring oil back to compressor
Low dome temperature with ON condition
codes.source: Trane System Alert Guide p.42, public distributor-posted PDF

platforms.research_note: Trane XV communicating outdoor platforms use structured alert IDs (CFG.005.01, ERR.188.05 format). Public OEM dictionary was not fully extractable in Pass 1 research — authoritative source is the Trane Technician app.

platforms.platform_name: Trane TAM8 / American Standard *AM8 Communicating Air Handler
platforms.platform_description: Hyperion-era communicating air handler family with comprehensive ERR code dictionary + 4-LED local diagnostic system + indoor electronic expansion valve (EEV) support + hydronic heat option (HHC) + electric heat control (EHC). Richest public indoor alert dictionary in the Trane ecosystem. Codes span communication faults (91, 159, 171), electric-heat faults (104, 105, 152, 153, 154), EEV faults (155, 160, 163, 164, 166), sensor faults (52, 161), configuration faults (114, 123, 160), and hydronic-heat faults (168, 169, 170, 173). Fault severity labels on the public page: Critical, User Display, Information.
platforms.visual_identification: Convertible air handler with four local LEDs on distribution board: COMM LED (amber) flashes count of communicating devices connected; BM LED (solid ON when powered); STATUS LED (flashes 1/sec in idle, flashes 1/100 CFM during demand); UNIT LED (blue, flashes once every 4 seconds when comm is normal). ERR codes display on thermostat (A/TCONT900, A/TZONE950). AFC STATUS LED solid-ON during certain faults.
platforms.platform_notes: Public source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, Alert Codes page 22. Multi-LED architecture means tech must read LEDs AND code together — either alone is incomplete. Subcodes like 91.02, 104.01, 114.05, 159.01 are stored as separate records. Fault levels from public page (Critical/User Display/Information) embedded in severity + escalation.

codes.meaning: Flashes the number of communicating devices connected on bus
diagnostic_path: Count flashes to verify expected number of devices connected
If count is wrong, check bus wiring and device power
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, Alert Codes, p.22

codes.meaning: Solid ON when power is applied to the air handler
diagnostic_path: If BM LED is OFF when power expected, check 24V supply and board power
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: Flashes once per second in Idle mode; during thermostat demand, flashes 1 time per 100 CFM requested. Note: AFC STATUS LED will be solid ON during certain faults.
diagnostic_path: Count flashes during demand to verify requested CFM matches thermostat call
Solid ON = fault condition; cross-reference active ERR code
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: Flashes once every four seconds when communications are normal
diagnostic_path: If UNIT LED not blinking or blinking irregularly, check ABCD bus communication
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: Control failure / Internal Control Error
diagnostic_path: Replace AFC per public field-reference page
codes.reset_behavior: Not separately stated in public row.
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: Twinning fault / Air handler twinning error
root_causes: More than one indoor unit connected on communicating bus — not allowed
diagnostic_path: Public page identifies multiple ID units on bus as the cause
Remove extra indoor unit from communicating bus
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when invalid twinning/bus configuration corrected.
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: Indoor air sensor error — ICAS OPEN
diagnostic_path: Check indoor air sensor circuit for open condition, unplugged sensor, or harness fault
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when indoor air sensor circuit returns to normal.
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: Indoor air sensor error — ICAS SHORT
diagnostic_path: Check indoor air sensor circuit for shorted sensor or shorted harness
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when indoor air sensor circuit returns to normal.
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: Communication busy fault — SYS COM CRC / SYS COMM
root_causes: R & B to thermostat reversed polarity
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when communication busy / CRC condition corrected.
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: Communication inactive fault / excessive communication errors
root_causes: R and B reversed polarity at thermostat
diagnostic_path: Verify thermostat polarity, data line, and CCM communications
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: Communication inactive fault / loss of heat-cool demand message
root_causes: Loss of Bit Master / communication source
diagnostic_path: Identify source of comm demand message loss on bus
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: Fuse failure / CHECK FUSE
diagnostic_path: Isolate 24V branches until short is found
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: Heater relay fault / EH RLY
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: Heater Relay Stuck Closed Error / EK RLY WELD#
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: All heater relay stuck open error / OPEN#
diagnostic_path: Check relay, heater element, and non-cycling limit
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: Heater interlock relay fault / blower interlock relay stuck open
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: Heater interlock relay fault / blower interlock relay stuck closed
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: External shutdown fault / EXT SW OPEN
root_causes: External float switch or other device open
diagnostic_path: Check external shutdown/float device and wiring
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when external shutdown input closes normally.
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: PM data corrupt / PM DATA ERR
root_causes: Incorrect VS motor or PM installed
diagnostic_path: Verify proper motor / personality module (PM) pairing
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: ID motor error / ID MTR ERR
root_causes: Motor HP value less than PM value
Incorrect VS motor or PM installed
diagnostic_path: Verify installed motor / PM data compatibility
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: PM bad or missing fault / PM MISSING
root_causes: PM missing with good local copy
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when PM presence/data issue corrected.
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: PM bad or missing fault / PM UNIT ERR
root_causes: Bad data in PM with good local copy
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when PM data issue corrected.
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: ID motor error / MTR PWR HI
root_causes: Motor HP is greater than PM value
Incorrect VS motor or PM installed
diagnostic_path: Verify installed motor horsepower against PM value
Confirm correct VS motor / PM pairing
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when motor / PM mismatch corrected.
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: Demand configuration fault / EH CONFIG
root_causes: New unprogrammed EHC installed with KW jumper on PARK
diagnostic_path: Set KW jumper to match model / heater configuration
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: Heater element fault / EH ELMNT OP#
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: Thermal limit switch fault / EH CTCO TRIP
root_causes: Cycling thermal cut-out (CTCO) trip has occurred
codes.escalation: Normal / trip event noted in public alert guide
codes.reset_behavior: Public alert-guide snippet says cycle power to clear this alert.
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22 and public alert-code snippet

codes.meaning: EH airflow limit fault / EH OFF/NO AF
root_causes: Electric heat control locked out because minimum airflow request has not been met
diagnostic_path: Verify external static / airflow table against minimum airflow requirement
codes.reset_behavior: Public alert-code snippet says cycle power to air handler to clear the alert.
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: EH airflow limit fault / EH LIM/NO AF
root_causes: Electric heat stages limited because minimum airflow cannot be met
diagnostic_path: Verify external static / airflow against required minimum
codes.escalation: Major — electric heat limited rather than fully locked out
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: Indoor EEV motor fault / EEV MTR ERR
root_causes: EEV motor fault detected for 8 consecutive times
diagnostic_path: Verify motor resistance against Service Facts
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: Indoor electronic expansion valve motor fault / EEV MTR ERR
root_causes: EEV electric motor current indicates open or short
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when EEV motor open/short fault corrected.
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: System low on charge fault / SYSTEM1 — high superheat condition
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: Condensate fault / ERR FLT
diagnostic_path: Check condensate overflow switch, line, and drainage
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: Coil freeze fault / ID COIL FRST
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: Unit Bus fault / AFC COMM
root_causes: Wire harness between AFC and distribution board
diagnostic_path: Inspect harness between AFC and distribution board
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: Unit Bus fault / EHC COMM
root_causes: Wire harness between EHC and distribution board
diagnostic_path: Inspect harness between EHC and distribution board
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: Unit Bus fault / HHC COMM
root_causes: Wire harness between HHC and distribution board
diagnostic_path: Inspect harness between HHC and distribution board
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: Unit Bus fault / EVC COMM
root_causes: Wire harness between EVC and distribution board
diagnostic_path: Inspect harness between EVC and distribution board
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: Unit Bus fault / CDA COMM — Display Assembly communication error
root_causes: Wire harness between Display Assembly and distribution board
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: Indoor EEV control configuration error / CONFIG FLT
root_causes: Configuration group index or evaporator temperature sensor type not configured correctly
PM programming or internal communication error
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: Evaporator temperature sensor fault / ET SEN ERR
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: Gas temperature sensor fault / GT SEN ERR
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: Indoor EEV control fault / EEV CNTL FLT
root_causes: EEV motor drive, feedback, or 12V failure detected
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when EEV control fault corrected.
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: Indoor EEV fault / EEV CLSE ERR
root_causes: EEV migrated to close position but superheat is not at desired setpoint
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when EEV response fault corrected.
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: Indoor EEV error / EEV OPEN ERR
root_causes: EEV motor at open position but valve/system response is incorrect
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when valve/system response returns normal.
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: Superheat is flooding fault / SYSTEM4 ERR
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when flooding/superheat control issue corrected.
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: OD data missing / outdoor status unknown to EVC
root_causes: If COMM LED on OD is flashing the correct device count, replace AFC
diagnostic_path: If OD COMM is flashing correct count, replace AFC
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when outdoor status becomes known to EVC.
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: Indoor heat demand is not available to hydronic heat control — HH FAULT
root_causes: Hydronic Heat is not controlling because indoor heat demand is not received
diagnostic_path: Verify hydronic heat control is receiving indoor heat demand on communicating system
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when indoor heat demand properly received by HHC.
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: Hydronic heat control configuration fault — HH CONFIG
root_causes: Hydronic heat capacity is unknown to hydronic heat control
Dip switch on HHC not set on replacement control only
diagnostic_path: Verify HHC dip-switch settings on replacement control
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: Hydronic heat control freeze condition detected — HH FRZ DET
root_causes: Hydronic heat coil freeze inlet temperature sensor reading is less than 38°F
diagnostic_path: Inspect for actual freeze condition at the hydronic coil
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when freeze condition / freeze-sensor returns to normal.
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: Communication inactive fault / BLW COMM — blower communication not detected
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: Display Assembly stuck key / KEY FAULT
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: Hydronic coil freeze inlet temperature sensor short — HH FRZ SHORT
diagnostic_path: Verify hydronic freeze sensor for short
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

codes.meaning: Hydronic coil freeze inlet temperature sensor open — HH FRZ OPEN
diagnostic_path: Verify hydronic freeze sensor for open
codes.source: Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data, p.22

cross_generation_conflicts.description: Code 6 meaning varies across all four Trane legacy flash-code platforms

meanings.meaning: 115V AC power reversed OR poor grounding
115V AC power reversed, poor grounding, OR system voltage too low
115V AC power reversed, ignitor (triac) fault, poor grounding, OR low voltage
Line reverse polarity only (narrowest)

cross_generation_conflicts.ui_banner_text: Code 6 on your Trane/American Standard furnace means different things depending on platform. On UD2/DD2 it's polarity or grounding. On UC1/DC1 it adds 'low voltage'. On AUY/TUY it also adds 'ignitor triac fault'. On AUH2/TUH2 it's only reverse polarity. Your model prefix determines the exact diagnosis.

cross_generation_conflicts.description: Code 9 has fundamentally different meaning on early vs later legacy platforms

meanings.meaning: Check ignitor circuit and Line N to 24VAC Common voltage — possible grounding problem
Check ignitor circuit and Line N to 24VAC Common voltage — possible grounding problem
Open inducer limit switch
Open inducer limit error

cross_generation_conflicts.ui_banner_text: Code 9 means completely different things across Trane/American Standard legacy platforms. On UD2/DD2 and UC1/DC1, it's an ignitor/grounding fault. On AUY/TUY and AUH2/TUH2, it's an open inducer limit — totally different diagnostic path. Your model prefix determines which it is.

cross_generation_conflicts.description: Code 10 exists only on AUY/TUY platform — not on AUH2/TUH2 despite shared architecture

meanings.meaning: Inducer communication error
Code 10 not present in published table — AUH2/TUH2 dictionary stops at Code 9

cross_generation_conflicts.ui_banner_text: Code 10 only exists on Trane/American Standard AUY/TUY platforms. If your AUH2/TUH2 is flashing 10, it's not a published code — check service documentation or verify flash count.

cross_generation_conflicts.description: Legacy Code 4 vs S-series E04: same concept but E04 broadened to include rollout and reverse-airflow

meanings.meaning: Open High Limit Device
Open Temperature Limit Circuit
Open Temperature Limit Switch
Open Limit or Rollout Switch
Open Limit (Main Thermal, Rollout, OR Reverse Airflow Switch) — broader scope

cross_generation_conflicts.ui_banner_text: Legacy Code 4 was 'open limit'. The newer S-series e04 includes rollout and reverse-airflow switches in the same code — diagnostic path must branch on which device opened.

cross_generation_conflicts.description: Legacy Code 6 combined polarity/grounding/voltage/triac — S-series splits into separate e6.1, e6.2, e6.3 subcodes

meanings.meaning: Combined: polarity OR grounding
Combined: polarity, grounding, or low voltage
Combined: polarity, triac, grounding, or low voltage
Polarity only
Split into 3 codes: e6.1 polarity, e6.2 grounding, e6.3 igniter relay/open

cross_generation_conflicts.ui_banner_text: Legacy Code 6 bundled multiple electrical issues into one code. The newer S-series splits the same territory into e6.1 (polarity), e6.2 (grounding), and e6.3 (igniter relay/open) — more precise diagnosis.

cross_generation_conflicts.description: Trane uses single Err.126.00 Equipment Change Alert vs Carrier's separate 'Indoor/Outdoor unit not found' commissioning codes

meanings.meaning: Equipment Change Alert — any enabled device that stopped reporting on bus (one code, read Summary Table to identify which device)

cross_generation_conflicts.ui_banner_text: Trane uses discovery-based fault detection via the wall-control Summary Table. Err.126.00 is a single code that fires whenever any enabled device goes offline on the communicating bus — unlike Carrier's separate 'Indoor/Outdoor unit not found' commissioning codes, you must read the Summary Table to identify which device is offline.

cross_generation_conflicts.description: Universal Hard Lockout 184.08 has DIFFERENT reset procedures depending on system generation

meanings.meaning: Clear by cycling power to outdoor unit OR clearing fault history in outdoor CDA
Clear by rebooting system from thermostat menu

cross_generation_conflicts.ui_banner_text: CRITICAL FIELD QUIRK: ERR.184.08 Universal Hard Lockout on XV20i/XV18 outdoor requires different reset procedures on Legacy vs Link systems. Using the wrong procedure leaves the hard lockout active even when the underlying condition has cleared. Confirm system generation before attempting reset.

electric_heat_notes.conventional_electric_furnace_codes: Trane/American Standard communicating air handlers (TAM8/*AM8) have the most developed electric-heat code coverage in this brand family. Key electric-heat-specific codes include ERR104.00/.01/.02 (heater relay faults), ERR105.00/.01 (heater interlock relay faults), ERR152.00 (heater element open), ERR153.00 (thermal limit / CTCO trip from low airflow), ERR154.00 (EH locked out - minimum airflow not met), ERR154.01 (EH limited - partial airflow). Non-communicating electric furnaces and legacy air handlers (GAF2, TEM6, older generations) use conventional sequencer/element logic without formal fault code dictionaries — service calls are component-level (open element, failed sequencer, limit trip, contactor).
common_non_code_electric_service_calls: Open element on multi-stage EH packages (3-stage, 5-stage, etc.)
Failed heater relay or sequencer — on TAM8 this will throw ERR104.xx
Airflow-related limit trip — on TAM8 this throws ERR153.00
Low-voltage wiring short causing repeated fuse failure
CTCO (cycle thermal cutout) trip from dirty coil/filter
electric_heat_notes.manufactured_home_specifics: Not applicable — Trane Technologies does not have a significant manufactured-home-specific product line.

metadata.source_note: Compiled from Trane installer/service documentation (18-CD19D2-18, 18-CD19D5-21, 18-CD20D3-21, 18-CD26D1-13, 18-CD29D1-14-EN, 18-CE04D1-1D-EN, 18-CE12D1-1D-EN, 18-CE19D1-1B-EN, 18-CE03D1-1F-EN, 18-HD80D1-6F-EN, 18-HD73D1-9E-EN, 11-HD15D1-5-EN), Trane System Alert Guide public distributor PDF, and Trane Hyperion *AM8/TAM8 Field Reference Data.
